description: Difficulty-stratified evaluation taxonomy for agent-modelica benchmarks. Stage subtypes describe where an error manifests in the compile/simulate pipeline; difficulty layers describe the task difficulty presented to the agent.

concepts.dominant_stage_subtype: Diagnostic-stage label derived from compiler/simulator evidence. This is not a difficulty label.
concepts.difficulty_layer: Benchmark difficulty label describing the agent-facing cognitive/control difficulty. This is not a raw diagnostic stage.

layer_1.label: Layer 1
layer_1.definition: Parse, syntax, undefined symbol, or shallow structural failures.

layer_2.label: Layer 2
layer_2.definition: Balance, reference, connector, and declarative consistency failures.

layer_3.label: Layer 3
layer_3.definition: Semantic, behavioral, or branch-sensitive failures where single-round LLM assistance still has a realistic chance.

layer_4.label: Layer 4
layer_4.definition: Initialization singularity, deep numerical instability, or structurally hard failures where single-round LLM usually fails and multi-round strategy or stronger tool guidance is typically required.

notes.stage_vs_layer: Stage and layer are related but not equivalent. The mapping above is a default only; future sidecar metadata may override it based on observed or inferred evidence.
